tasks.notes: Validation package scaffold exists; implementation instructions and gates are repository-local.
Finite-observer physical state ontology is specified and verified over the 256-state admissible hyperplane.
Lazy pair-flip microscopic dynamics, continuous-time generator, and exact pair-flip graph spectrum are implemented and verified.

Finite event graph, graph-distance bound, pair-flip degree, edge count, spectral gap, and R-011 nested finite causal hierarchy are specified and tested. R-011 closes only the finite-observer route; no physical light cone, relativistic metric, or observed spacetime propagation is derived.
Dimensionless finite-observer bridge observables are implemented and tested.

A finite-observer substitute replaces an unclaimed continuum limit. R-011 adds nested parity-valid observer levels, parity-preserving projective maps, uniform fibers, finite causal cones, event non-expansion, and normalized scale annotations with deterministic tests and artifacts. No differentiable continuum, Lorentzian metric, Einstein equation, FRW/LCDM dynamics, or external observational validation is derived.

Finite Hamming-weight background surrogates remain implemented on the nine-cube state geometry. R-012 adds an explicit synthetic finite-observer cosmological background-equation workbench, normalized ASH source, exact Omega_ASH=0 standard-baseline relation, deterministic artifacts, and targeted tests. This is not empirical cosmology, not a physical FRW continuum, not an Einstein-equation derivation, not an external likelihood, not a CMB/matter-spectrum solver, and not a locked prediction.

Finite perturbation-mode decay factors remain implemented. R-013 adds a bounded finite-to-physical scalar-sector map, Newtonian-gauge sub-horizon matter-growth equation, executable RK4 solver, matter-power proxy, low-ell CMB interface proxy, deterministic synthetic alpha_mu recovery, and generated validation artifacts. Relativistic metric-sector derivations, a full photon-baryon Boltzmann hierarchy, CLASS/CAMB replacement, external likelihoods, empirical CMB or matter validation, and locked predictions remain outside scope.

Internal finite-observer observable vector, affine calibration contract, R-010 synthetic finite-observer unit-bearing bridge, and R-014 metadata-only external likelihood readiness contracts are implemented and tested. R-010 uses fiducial synthetic scales, and R-014 uses synthetic fixtures only; neither supplies reviewed physical calibration, official external data scoring, empirical validation, or a locked scientific prediction.
Finite synthetic consistency gate is implemented through background-kernel lumping tests.

Diagonal Gaussian likelihood and model-comparison mechanics remain implemented. R-014 adds external-likelihood readiness with Cholesky covariance handling, metadata-only reviewed product registry contracts, preregistration/hash policy, matched synthetic baselines, and deterministic synthetic recovery validation. Official external data vectors, official covariance files, observed-data scoring, empirical validation, and locked predictions remain outside scope.
Finite exact-matrix solver gates are implemented; continuum convergence remains unclaimed.

Dimensionless flat standard-baseline comparator remains implemented. R-012 adds an exact Omega_ASH=0 standard-baseline relation for the synthetic finite-observer background workbench, with generated validation JSON and regression tests. External empirical baseline matching remains open.
Prediction ledger hash-lock mechanics and gate validation are implemented; no scientific prediction is currently locked.

Finite-observer theorem catalog and proof obligations include exact nine-cube geometry, pair-flip graph geometry, finite background moments, R-010 unit-bridge checks, R-011 finite-observer checks, the R-012 synthetic background-equation standard-baseline identity, R-013 bounded perturbation workbench evidence, and R-014 external-likelihood readiness evidence.
Claim and strict repository scanners are present and wired into CI and the repository gate.
Repository CI runs verification, artifact generation, proof, test, language, schema, and consistency gates.

open_blockers: No reviewed physical calibration constants map finite ASH observables to measured spacetime, fields, or data products; R-010 uses fiducial synthetic scales only.
No metric, light-cone, or relativistic spacetime interpretation is derived from the finite event graph; R-011 closes only the finite-observer limit route.

No unit-bearing bridge with reviewed physical calibration, official external covariance source, observed-data matched baseline, and prediction lock is committed; R-010, R-012, R-013, and R-014 are synthetic finite-observer, matter-sector, or readiness workbenches only.
No gauge convention or physical degrees-of-freedom map exists for a differentiable continuum interpretation.

No ASH-derived standard cosmological background with reviewed physical FRW or Einstein-equation status is committed; R-012 closes only a synthetic finite-observer background-equation workbench and standard-baseline relation.

No full photon-baryon Boltzmann hierarchy, CLASS/CAMB replacement, survey-calibrated CMB or matter-spectrum observable solver, or external perturbation likelihood is committed; R-013 closes only a bounded matter-sector workbench and proxy interface.
No external data product, official covariance source, or observed-data matched empirical baseline is committed; R-014 supplies metadata-only registry/contracts and synthetic fixtures only.
No scientific prospective or held-out prediction is locked.
claim_downgrades: ASH-Physics v0.2 completes a finite-observer stochastic foundation only.
Exact hypercube and pair-flip graph spectra are finite state-geometry results, not a physical metric or cosmological expansion law.
R-012 supplies a synthetic finite-observer background-equation workbench and exact standard-baseline relation; it is not empirical cosmology or a physical continuum-gravity derivation.
Finite background and perturbation equations are internal or synthetic workbench equations, not empirical cosmology.
R-010 supplies a synthetic unit-bearing bridge workbench with fiducial scales; it is not reviewed physical calibration or empirical validation.
R-011 supplies a finite-observer hierarchy and finite causal graph workbench; it is not a differentiable continuum, Lorentzian metric, physical light cone, or empirical cosmology result.
Calibration and likelihood mechanics are contracts; they are not external empirical validation.
External validation remains blocked until reviewed physical calibrations, external data products, matched baselines, and locked scientific predictions exist.

R-013 supplies a bounded matter-sector perturbation workbench and low-ell CMB interface proxy; it is not a full Boltzmann solver, empirical CMB or matter-spectrum validation, external likelihood, or locked prediction.
R-014 supplies external-likelihood readiness with metadata-only reviewed product contracts and synthetic fixtures; it is not observed-data scoring, empirical validation, or a locked prediction.
